skill.name: material-component-dev
skill.description: Guides creation of FlowGram form-materials React components with exports, Storybook examples, and type-check steps.
skill.summary: Create FlowGram form-materials components using the project conventions.
skill.author: bytedance

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported many command, network, filesystem, and weak-crypto patterns, but review found them in Markdown documentation and code examples. No malicious intent, credential access, exfiltration, prompt injection, or executable installer script was found in SKILL.md.

low_findings.title: Documented Local Development Commands
low_findings.description: The command-related static findings are Markdown inline text and fenced examples for mkdir, yarn ts-check, rush build:watch, and yarn dev. They are user-visible local project commands, not hidden Ruby backtick execution or dynamic command execution in skill code.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The referenced lines are Markdown instructions and shell examples for normal FlowGram development. There is no evidence of concealed execution, shell interpolation, or remote command loading.

low_findings.title: Localhost Storybook URL
low_findings.description: The hardcoded URL points to http://localhost:6006/ for manual Storybook testing. It does not send data to an external service and is expected for local development documentation.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The URL is explicitly localhost and appears in a testing step. No evidence found of network exfiltration or calls to a third-party endpoint.

low_findings.title: Relative Import Path Traversal Flag
low_findings.description: The path traversal finding is caused by a TypeScript relative import reference to ../code-editor in the technical stack notes. It is not file access code and does not read or write arbitrary paths.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The suspicious sequence is inside a documented import path for an internal component. There is no path concatenation, file API call, or user-controlled filesystem access.

low_findings.title: Weak Cryptography Keyword False Positive
low_findings.description: The weak-cryptography findings map to ordinary words in the description and Storybook metadata, not cryptographic API use. No hashing, encryption, or digest implementation appears at these locations.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited lines contain a frontmatter description and a Storybook description property. No evidence found of MD5, SHA1, DES, RC4, or similar weak algorithms being used.

content.value_statement: FlowGram contributors need consistent component structure, exports, and Storybook coverage. This skill guides component creation in form-materials with TypeScript and project-specific conventions.
actual_capabilities: Explains where to create new components in the FlowGram form-materials package.
Provides TypeScript component, helper, modal, and index export patterns.
Shows how to add package-level exports for new material components.
Provides a Storybook story template for manual component testing.
Lists common FlowGram component errors and suggested fixes.
Defines acceptance criteria for exports, props, type checks, and Storybook behavior.
limitations: Targets FlowGram form-materials only, not arbitrary React projects.
Does not include automated unit test guidance for new components.
Assumes the repository already has Rush, Yarn, Storybook, and FlowGram packages installed.
Provides templates and conventions, but does not validate final component behavior automatically.

use_cases.title: Create a New Form Material
use_cases.description: Use the guide to place files, define props, export the component, and prepare a Storybook story.

use_cases.title: Align Component Code With Project Rules
use_cases.description: Check named exports, explicit React imports, Semi UI usage, and FlowGram internal type imports.

use_cases.title: Debug Integration Errors
use_cases.description: Use the FAQ to resolve missing exports, React UMD errors, schema type issues, and editor layout problems.

prompt_templates.title: Plan a Component
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner planning a new material component
prompt_templates.prompt: Help me plan a new FlowGram form-materials component named [ComponentName]. Define its core purpose, minimal props, files, exports, and Storybook story.

prompt_templates.title: Generate Component Files
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a FlowGram form-materials component named [ComponentName] using TypeScript, Semi UI, named exports, explicit React imports, and minimal props.

prompt_templates.title: Add Storybook Coverage
prompt_templates.scenario: Prepare manual testing for the component
prompt_templates.prompt: Add a Storybook story for [ComponentName] in the demo-materials app. Include realistic state, callback handling, and docs metadata.

prompt_templates.title: Review Integration Readiness
prompt_templates.prompt: Review my FlowGram component changes for export completeness, prop simplicity, internal dependency usage, type-check readiness, and Storybook usability.

output_examples.input: Plan a new JSON helper component for form-materials.
output: A concise component purpose and prop list.
A recommended folder structure under the form-materials components directory.
Export and Storybook steps required for integration.

output_examples.input: Review why my new component is undefined in Storybook.
output: A checklist of component-level, package-level, and root exports to verify.
Likely causes for undefined React components.
Next validation steps using local type checks and Storybook.

output_examples.input: Prepare acceptance criteria for a new FlowGram material component.
output: Named export and type export requirements.
Manual Storybook and type-check verification steps.

best_practices: Keep each material component focused on one clear task with minimal required props.
Use FlowGram internal components and types before adding external dependencies.
Validate exports, type checks, and Storybook behavior before review.
anti_patterns: Creating a separate package for a component that belongs in form-materials.
Using default exports when the package expects named exports.
Adding broad configuration props before the core component behavior is proven.

faq.question: What project is this skill for?
faq.answer: It is for FlowGram form-materials component development inside the FlowGram repository.

faq.question: Does it generate production-ready code by itself?
faq.answer: It provides templates and review guidance. Developers still need to implement behavior and test the result.

faq.question: Which UI library does it expect?
faq.answer: It expects Semi UI through @douyinfe/semi-ui and FlowGram internal editor components when needed.

faq.question: Why does it require named exports?
faq.answer: The guide uses named exports to match package conventions and support tree shaking.

faq.question: How should a new component be tested?
faq.answer: The skill recommends type checking and manual testing through the demo-materials Storybook app.

faq.question: Does the skill access external services?
faq.answer: No evidence found of external service access. The only URL in the skill is a localhost Storybook address.
